description: Catalogue of financial instruments used by the inheritance-split sample: 30 Swiss-priority equities (SIX tickers + full company names + is-symbol-for links), 5 bonds (Swiss, German, US), 7 trading currencies (Germany-centric), and each security's main trading currency. Companion files: portfolio_split_calc.json (algorithm + formulas), portfolio_report.json (bank-reported holdings, prices, FX rates), portfolio_split_parameters_sample.json (concrete split agreement and sample allocations).

words.name: company
words.description: legal entity made up of an association of people for the purpose of carrying on a commercial or industrial enterprise

words.name: security
words.description: tradable financial asset of any kind, including equities, bonds and money-market instruments

words.name: stock
words.description: fractional ownership of a corporation, typically traded as shares on a stock exchange

words.name: bond
words.description: fixed-income debt security under which the issuer owes the holders a debt and is obliged to pay interest and repay the principal at maturity

words.name: cash
words.description: money in the physical form of currency, or balances on demand at a bank, available for immediate spending

words.name: currency
words.description: system of money in common use within a specific environment, especially for people in a nation state

words.name: main
words.description: qualifier denoting the primary or principal one in a set (e.g. the listing currency used most often for a security)

words.name: trading
words.description: of or relating to the buying and selling of securities

triples.name: trading currency
triples.description: the currency in which a security is bought, sold and quoted on its primary exchange

triples.name: main trading currency
triples.description: for a security listed on several venues, the trading currency of its primary listing; here used to tag every stock and bond with the currency its prices and nominal value are quoted in
